Hi Danilo, let us plan the week. The team meeting is on Monday at ten o clock.
Ten o clock, perfect. And the client call?
The client call is on Wednesday, at half past two.
Got it. When is the report deadline?
The deadline is on Friday, at noon. Please send it early.
No problem. And our one-on-one?
Our appointment is on Thursday, in the afternoon, at a quarter past four.
Let me add it to my calendar. A quarter past four.
Great. Remember: please be on time for the team meeting.
Always. I am never late. Thanks, Rachel.
The meeting is on Monday at ten o clock.
The deadline is on Friday at noon.
What time is the call? It is half past two.
I have an appointment in the afternoon.
Please be on time. I am never late.

Hi Danilo, it's Rachel. Just a quick reminder about your schedule this week. The team meeting is on Monday at ten o'clock, not at nine. On Wednesday, we have the client call at half past two. And please remember: the project report deadline is on Friday, at noon. One more thing: your one-on-one with me is on Thursday, in the afternoon, at a quarter past four. Please be on time. See you on Monday. Bye!

I am a data engineering lead, and my week is very busy. I start work early, at eight o'clock in the morning. On Mondays, we have our team meeting at nine. I check the calendar every day, so I am never late for an appointment. We have many deadlines: most reports are due on Friday, at noon. In the afternoon, I answer emails and help my team. I finish at six o'clock in the evening. I like to be on time, so I always plan my day. A good calendar is the secret of a calm week.
